User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the SwiftRoute application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the user entity.
Unique identifier for the shipment entity.

trackingNumber.description: The tracking number associated with the shipment.

status.description: Current status of the shipment (e.g., 'In Transit', 'Delivered').

id.description: Unique identifier for the flight manifest.

flightNumber.description: The flight number associated with the manifest.

PreAlert.title: PreAlert
PreAlert.description: Represents a pre-alert request with receipt image.

submissionDate.description: Date when the pre-alert was submitted.

deliveryAddressSuggestion.description: Suggested delivery address extracted from the receipt (AI powered).

shipmentId.description: Reference to Shipment if a shipment was created.

ProfitLossStatement.title: ProfitLossStatement
ProfitLossStatement.description: Represents a profit and loss statement.

id.description: Unique identifier for the profit loss statement.

statementDate.description: The date of the profit/loss statement.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to prioritize security, scalability, and debuggability, adhering to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC, and QAPs. It uses path-based ownership for user-related data and denormalization for collaborative data access. It also implements structural segregation such as storing admin users in a separate collection.

**Authorization Independence**: Achieved primarily through path-based ownership for private user data (Shipments, PreAlerts).  Admin roles are managed via document existence in `/roles_admin/{uid}`. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules for authorization, enabling atomic operations.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters)**: Achieved by segregating admin roles into `/roles_admin/{uid}`. List operations against `/users/{userId}/shipments` are secured because only the specified user's shipments are stored there. The structure avoids mixing data with different security requirements within the same collection. Enforcing this homogenous security posture for any collection enables the QAPs.

**Admin Login**: Admin users are stored as documents in the `/roles_admin/{userId}` collection. This enables distinct security rules for admin users. The existence of the admin documents in the `roles_admin` collection is used to authorize admin priviliges. This is preferred over storing admin roles as custom claims.

**User Signup**: Regular users are managed through Firebase Authentication and their profiles are stored in `/users/{userId}`.  Path based ownership is enabled.
